# Peninsula Insider — House Style Guide

**Owner:** Vera
**Voice principles owner:** Tyler (any voice change requires Tyler sign-off)
**Last consolidated:** 2026-04-16
**Rulings added since last consolidation:** 0

---

## 1. Voice Principles

*Quoted from Tyler's standing themes. Do not edit without Tyler sign-off.*

- The reader is brilliant and busy. Every weak sentence is a tax on them.
- We earn attention; we don't demand it.
- A good magazine has a point of view; a great one has standards.
- Individual section voices flex; the publication's centre of gravity holds.
- We write with taste, not performance.

## 2. Terminology

*How PI uses specific words. Updated when rulings arise.*

| Use | Don't use | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| winery | wine estate, wine house | "Estate" misleads on ownership of land vs operation |
| maker / producer | artisan, artisanal | "Artisan" has become marketing language |
| restaurant | eatery, venue, establishment | Plain English wins |
| cook / chef | culinarian | Plain English wins |
| Peninsula | the Peninsula, Mornington Peninsula (sparingly) | First reference spells out; subsequent "the Peninsula" |
| cafe | café | Accents only where the word demands it; cafe is English usage |
| sourdough | artisan loaf, craft bread | Specific word exists; use it |
| event | happening, experience | "Experience" is marketing |
| run down / well-worn | authentic, rustic | Describe, don't label |

## 3. Names & Titles

- **People on first reference:** full name + role + affiliation (e.g., "Luc Martin, owner of Main Street Bakery in Balnarring")
- **Subsequent reference:** surname (traditional magazine style). Exception: food and chef profiles where first-name-only may suit the voice — section editor decides.
- **Businesses:** exactly as they spell their own name. Check the signage or the website.
- **No honorifics** (no Mr/Mrs/Ms/Dr) except in quotes.
- **Indigenous names and terms:** follow current Indigenous Australian usage guides; when in doubt, ask the subject.

## 4. Numbers & Measurements

- **Numbers under 10:** words ("seven loaves")
- **Numbers 10+:** numerals ("47 producers")
- **Except** at sentence start — always words.
- **Ages:** "47 years old" (or "she is 47"); no "47 y/o."
- **Currency:** `$25` (no space, no `.00`); `$1,500` with comma for thousands.
- **Dates:** "Saturday 7 March" (day first in running text); "7 March 2026" (formal); "Saturday" only when obvious within the week.
- **Times:** "8am," "7.30pm" (lowercase, no space, period not colon).
- **Measurements:** metric, always. Hectares, kilometres, grams.

## 5. Formatting

- **Oxford comma:** yes, when it disambiguates; no when it doesn't. Vera calls it.
- **Em dashes:** use spaced ( — ) not unspaced. Sparingly.
- **Italics:** publication names, foreign words on first use only (subsequent: roman), *emphasis* (very sparingly — specificity should do the work).
- **Quotation marks:** double for speech; single for quotes within quotes. No "scare quotes" on normal nouns.
- **Subheads:** used in pieces >1200 words. Sentence case. No title case.
- **Lists:** use sparingly; prose preferred unless the content is genuinely enumerable.

## 6. Structure Norms

- **Feature length targets:** 700 / 1200 / 1800 / 2400 words. Nothing outside these without Margot sign-off.
- **Paragraph length:** varies; average 3–5 sentences; single-sentence paragraphs earn their place.
- **Lede discipline:** first paragraph is a specific detail, a moment, a person, not a summary or a rhetorical question.
- **End discipline:** piece ends; it does not merely stop. Last sentence carries weight.

## 7. Inclusion & Accessibility

- **Alt text:** every published image has descriptive alt text. "Photograph of a hand kneading dough on a floured wooden bench" — not "bread."
- **Plain-language rule:** any specialist term (winemaking, cooking technique, arts vocabulary) is defined on first use or used in context so the reader can infer.
- **Sensitive subjects:** mental health, grief, trauma, addiction — follow current Mindframe guidelines; consult Margot if unsure.
- **Representation:** actively consider whose stories PI covers; section editors are accountable for range.

## 8. Disclosure

- **Advertorial / branded content:** labelled at the top of the piece as "In partnership with [brand]" or "Sponsored." No ambiguous framing.
- **Personal relationships:** disclosed either in the piece (for material relationships) or flagged in the brief (for minor ones). Section editors do not cover close family members or current collaborators.
- **Hospitality (food):** Lucien's rule — accepts seat at bar; declines complimentary tastings worth the piece. Other writers follow the same rule unless Margot grants exception for a specific piece.
- **Hospitality (culture):** Iris's rule — attends as press only when the organiser would let any press in; does not accept perks a paying audience wouldn't.
- **Paid work:** writers do not cover any subject they've done paid work for within 12 months.

## 9. Rulings Log

*Chronological record. Monthly, Vera consolidates these into the sections above.*

### YYYY-WW{WW} — {Question title}
- **Question:** {What arose.}
- **Ruling:** {The call.}
- **Reasoning:** {Why.}
- **Generalises to:** {How far it applies.}

*(Example entry — delete when seeding)*

### 2026-W17 — "winery" vs "vineyard" vs "wine estate"
- **Question:** Lucien piece used "wine estate" for a producer who doesn't own the land.
- **Ruling:** Use "winery" for the production operation; "vineyard" for land/grapegrowing only. Do not use "wine estate" — misleads on ownership structure.
- **Reasoning:** Accurate terminology matters in a region where land ownership, grape sourcing, and winemaking operations often separate.
- **Generalises to:** all food-and-drink writing. Update section 2 of this guide at next consolidation.

---

## 10. Corrections Log

*Published corrections, with piece slug and what was changed. Anonymised as to writer.*

### {YYYY-MM-DD} — {piece slug}
- **What was changed:** {specific}
- **Why:** {specific}
- **Correction note appended:** ✓

## Meta

This guide lives in the PI runtime at `peninsula-insider/.claude/newsroom/house-style.md` — NOT in `04-agents/peninsula-insider/`. The template here is a seed; the active guide evolves with the content.

When Vera seeds the live guide from this template, she drops the "template — delete when seeding" markers and the example ruling.
